inputs.label: Incoming Emails
inputs.description: Emails from consultants, contractors, and project authorities — each with a different format, attachment type, and level of detail — land in the shared project inbox.
inputs.inputFormat: Emails with varied free-text bodies and mixed attachment types (PDF, CAD, scanned documents).

persona.quote: I get dozens of emails a week from different consultants — tracking which ones need a ticket and making sure attachments are filed correctly takes half my day.

stations.label: CAPTURE
stations.description: Ingests each email and confirms it belongs to an active project.

agents.name: Email Review
agents.description: Analyses the email content, validates the sender, and confirms the active project context.
agents.output: A verified email bound to the right project.

stations.label: UNDERSTAND
stations.description: Classifies the content, extracts attachment metadata, and retrieves the project's ISO 19650 taxonomy.

agents.name: Content Extraction
agents.description: Reads all attachments across mixed file formats — PDF, CAD, scanned documents — and extracts the raw content for further processing.
agents.output: Extracted text and data from all attachments, ready for classification.

agents.name: SGBuildex
agents.description: Queries the SGBuildex API to retrieve the HDB-specific ISO 19650 document naming convention and folder taxonomy for this project.
agents.output: The applicable ISO 19650 taxonomy: naming template, folder path, and required metadata columns.

agents.name: Classification
agents.description: Classifies the document type, engineering discipline, and project location using the extracted content and ISO 19650 taxonomy.
agents.output: A tagged item: document type, discipline, location.

stations.label: STRUCTURE
stations.description: Creates a tracked issue in Autodesk and files attachments to the ISO 19650 standard.

agents.name: Ticketing
agents.description: Creates a typed Autodesk Issue from the email, linked to the relevant project element and assignee.
agents.output: An Autodesk Issue with type, discipline, description, and project linkage.

agents.name: Document Archival
agents.description: Files each attachment into the correct folder with an ISO 19650-compliant name and all required metadata columns, following the taxonomy retrieved from SGBuildex.
agents.output: Attachments archived under compliant names, in the correct folder, with full metadata.

stations.label: GOVERN
stations.description: Archives the email thread as a traceable decision record in the project's Common Data Environment.

agents.name: Decision Record
agents.description: Archives the email thread into a SharePoint document library as an ISO 19650 decision record — capturing who decided what, when, and based on which documents. Indexed for Copilot Agent to interrogate the project's decision trail.
agents.output: An ISO 19650 decision record in SharePoint: email thread, linked documents, and decision context — queryable by Copilot for decision tracking and audit.

outputs.label: Automated Ticketing
outputs.description: A typed, linked issue created on the Autodesk platform for project tracking and follow-up.

outputs.label: Compliant Archiving
outputs.description: Attachments archived in the correct folder with ISO 19650-compliant names and full metadata.

outputs.label: Decision Traceability
outputs.description: The email thread indexed in SharePoint as an ISO 19650 decision record — traceable and queryable by Copilot.

steps.caption: An HDB project manager tracks dozens of emails a week — manual triage, renaming, and filing consume half their day.
Emails arrive from multiple stakeholders — varied formats, attachment types, and levels of detail.
The Email Review Agent ingests each email and confirms project context.
Classification, Document Analysis, and SGBuildex Agents tag the content and retrieve the ISO 19650 taxonomy.
The Ticketing Agent raises an Autodesk Issue; the Document Archival Agent files attachments to spec.
The Decision Record Agent archives the email thread to SharePoint as an ISO 19650 decision record.
A typed Autodesk Issue is created and linked to the project.
Attachments are filed under ISO 19650-compliant names with full metadata.
The email thread is preserved as a decision record — Copilot can now trace who decided what and why.
